metricComparisons.description: higher modulate depth on the osc source should drive a clearly larger frame-to-frame warp than the dry baseline
the stronger modulate setting should visibly deepen the warp relative to the lighter setting

expectedVideo: raising modulate amount should increase UV warp without tearing or black frames
the visual motion should remain phase-coherent rather than jittering randomly
expectedAudio: raising modulate amount should deepen phase modulation and sidebands without collapsing pitch identity
output should stay controlled across the sweep with no obvious crackle or level jumps
manualChecks: Does PM depth increase feel proportional to the visual warp depth?
Does the higher setting stay usable for professional sound design rather than sounding broken?
Does the stronger modulate setting clearly change the rendered oscillator timbre by ear even though a monotonic spectral-centroid lift is not stable enough to hard-gate?
